metadata.title: How to outsource everything to AI & get dumb:
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: this is Liam, in for Bear. GPS can weaken spatial memory: rely on it for every trip and familiar streets become difficult without it. AI is doing the same thing to your thinking. But there's a way to use AI without outsourcing your brain. It costs two extra minutes on the front. It saves hours on the back. And the work that comes out is yours, not Claude's.

props.segment: How to outsource everything to AI & get dumb:
props.command: how do I use AI without letting it make me dumb?

beats.narration_text: Here's how most people use AI — and why it fails them. You face a problem. You open Claude. You paste it in and hope Claude understands what you meant. It doesn't. Not exactly. Just like GPS, it'll get you somewhere. But you stop looking up. You stop knowing the terrain. And the next time you have to navigate without it, you're lost in your own city.

props.artifactTitle: How most people use AI (brain rot edition)
artifactLines: Face problem → open Claude → paste it in → hope it understands.
Claude produces something. You ship it. The client isn't happy.
You fix it. You cross-check every formula. You rewrote the email 3 times.
You saved time by using AI. You wasted time fixing AI.

beats.narration_text: The method draws one line: you can outsource work to AI. You cannot outsource understanding. These are not the same thing. Outsourcing the production of a document is fine. Outsourcing the thinking that should have happened before you opened Claude — that's how you rot. The difference is whether you arrive at the prompt with a clear intent or you arrive hoping Claude figures it out.

props.artifactTitle: The one line you can't cross
artifactLines: ✓ Outsource: writing, formatting, first drafts, synthesis.
✗ Outsource: deciding what the goal is.
✗ Outsource: knowing whether the output is right.
Clear intent in → useful output out. Vague hope in → AI slop out.

beats.narration_text: Consider a Chief of Staff at a two-hundred-forty-person company. The request is: I need to roll out Claude across the whole org — help me sell you internally. By Friday she needed three things: a ninety-day adoption roadmap, a one-page pricing breakdown, and an email she could send her exec team. Her credibility partially on the line.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B03.png (diagram of the Chief of Staff scenario or the five-step method)

beats.narration_text: The method is five steps. Step one: before touching Claude, write your constraints. What's the output format? Who reads it? What can't be in it? Step two: write your first draft — even a bad one — before asking Claude to improve it. Step three: paste your draft plus your constraints. Step four: ask Claude for three versions. Step five: pick one, edit it, call it yours. Two extra minutes of thinking. The work that comes out is yours.

props.title: no-brain-rot-method.md
props.code: # 5 Steps to outsource work, not thinking 1. Write your constraints before opening Claude. (output format, audience, what can't be in it) 2. Write a rough draft — even a bad one. 3. Paste: draft + constraints → Claude. 4. Ask for 3 versions. 5. Pick one. Edit it. That's yours.

beats.narration_text: The method applies across three very different types of work. A strategic document — like the ninety-day roadmap — where you write the goals and constraints first. A communication piece — like an exec email — where you write the key point before Claude writes the sentences. And a spreadsheet — where you list the assumptions before Claude builds. Same method, three domains, zero brain rot.

artifactLines: Strategy doc → write goals and constraints → Claude drafts → you edit.
Executive email → write the one point that must land → Claude writes the words.
Spreadsheet → list assumptions for Claude to sanity-check → Claude builds.

beats.narration_text: The trap is subtle. The brain rot doesn't feel like getting dumb. It feels like getting fast. You're shipping things faster, answering faster, producing more. But when someone asks you to explain the reasoning behind the document — you can't. You used AI to think so you don't have to. And the first time AI isn't available, or the client pushes back, you realize you don't know the terrain anymore.

shot.sparkLine: Fast feels fine. Until it doesn't.
shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B06.png (the diagram or quote card from the article about the difference between outsourcing work vs. understanding)

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Pick one deliverable you're working on this week. Write your constraints and your rough intent before you open Claude. Then paste this prompt to let Claude work within your thinking, not instead of it.

props.segment: How to outsource everything to AI & get dumb:
props.command: Here is my rough draft and my constraints: [paste draft] [paste constraints: audience, format, what can't be in it]. Give me 3 improved versions. Keep my core point. Don't change my voice.

beats.narration_text: Don't outsource your thinking. Just your typing. Nik Bear Brown.

props.title: How to outsource everything to AI & get dumb:
props.subline: Draft first. Claude improves. You decide.
